channel.title: Cold War on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Cold War on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Ghost rockets
item.description:
Ghost rockets were rocket or missile-shaped unidentified flying objects first sighted in 1946, primarily in Sweden and other Scandinavian countries. While many sightings were attributed to meteors, some displayed characteristics inconsistent with natural phenomena, such as reported maneuverability. Investigations considered origins from former German rocket facilities, with theories suggesting Soviet tests of captured German missiles. However, some military opinions, including a declassified USAF document, suggested the possibility of extraterrestrial origins for these and later flying saucer reports. Similar sightings were also reported in Greece, Portugal, Belgium, and Italy, with investigations in Greece being halted by the army after conferring with foreign officials.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ghost_rockets

item.title: Lenticular Reentry Vehicle
item.description:
The Lenticular Reentry Vehicle (LRV) was a secret Cold War-era project developed by North American Aviation under the management of Wright-Patterson Air Force Base. Declassified documents from 1999 describe the craft as a 40-foot, saucer-shaped vessel designed for crewed orbital missions, though there is no official confirmation that it ever flew. While some have linked the project to military flying saucer theories, technical analysis indicates that the LRV functioned more like a conventional space capsule than the anomalous craft frequently reported in UFO sightings. Research into the vehicle grew out of previous initiatives like the Pye Wacket project, which explored the feasibility of lenticular-shaped defensive systems.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lenticular_Reentry_Vehicle

item.title: UFO Reports and Atomic Sites
item.description:
Since 1947, US media has speculated about a link between UFO reports and atomic sites. Early in the Cold War, domestic UFO reports were taken seriously, with some media suggesting they were related to atomic energy transmutation, a claim later dismissed. By the 1990s, legends arose of UFOs interfering with missile tests and nuclear complexes. Statistical studies suggest UFO reports are more common near military or atomic sites, though experts argue this may be due to increased scrutiny of these areas.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UFO_reports_and_atomic_sites

item.title: America's Nuclear Flying Saucer
item.description:
The Lenticular Reentry Vehicle (LRV) was an experimental nuclear warhead delivery system developed during the Cold War by North American Aviation. Research into lenticular-shaped vehicles for re-entry characteristics began in the late 1950s, with the LRV project being classified in 1962 and declassified in 1999. The LRV was described as a 40-foot half-saucer capable of supporting a crew of four for six-week orbital missions, powered by a rocket engine and an onboard nuclear reactor. While its existence may lend credence to military flying saucer theories, its described flight characteristics are more akin to a standard space capsule.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lenticular_Reentry_Vehicle
